source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design the base frame of a Mobile Launch Tower for a small-launcher launch-site benchmark as a welded/bolted multi-part steel assembly consisting of four outer UC-section columns, diagonal CHS tube cross-bracing, horizontal CHS tube cross-bracing at base level, a four-corner base gusset plate set welded between columns and braces, four outer umbilical-arm tip attachment brackets where hold-down loads enter the tower top, and 12 M48 Grade 10.9 anchor bolts into the launch-site concrete mat. The tower supports a 50 t launch vehicle (fueled) at T-0 and must carry a 1 MN hold-down force per umbilical arm with four arms total (4 MN total hold-down). Tower overall height 30 m, base footprint 8 m x 8 m square. Base frame primary material: AS/NZS 3679.1 Grade 350 structural steel (Young's modulus 200 GPa, Poisson 0.30, yield 350 MPa, ultimate 450 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3) rolled sections. Four outer columns built from UC 310 x 283 kg/m W-sections; cross-bracing from CHS 200 x 8 mm tubes. Base anchor bolts: 12 x M48 Grade 10.9 into launch-site concrete mat. Load cases per Eurocode EN 1990 + EN 1991-1-4 wind + ISO 19901 offshore-ish launch environment: (LC1) dead load: tower self-weight 200 t + launch vehicle 50 t = 2450 kN vertical on base frame; (LC2) pre-launch hold-down: 4 MN upward at the four umbilical arm tips + 2450 kN down = 1550 kN net upward on base; (LC3) ignition transient: 1 MN horizontal blast load on one side of tower for 500 ms; (LC4) wind 50 m/s ULS (1/100 yr): 1.0 kPa dynamic pressure x 30 m tower x ~8 m projected = 240 kN horizontal at mid-height; (LC5) seismic 0.2 g horizontal per site design spectrum; (LC6) T-0 release: 4 MN hold-down instantaneous release = 4 MN downward rebound on base for 100 ms. Pass/fail per EN 1993-1 (γM0 = 1.00, γM2 = 1.25): (R1) max von Mises in W-sections under LC1-LC5 <= 350 MPa (γM0 yield); (R2) base anchor bolt tension under LC2 + LC3 <= 660 kN per bolt (M48 Grade 10.9 tensile capacity 910 kN / 1.38 ULS combined with shear); (R3) horizontal deflection at tower top under LC4 wind <= H/200 = 150 mm; (R4) base frame mass <= 50 t; (R5) first sway mode above 2 Hz to stay clear of ignition transient excitation; (R6) linear buckling eigenvalue of columns under LC2 upward reaction >= 2.5. Verification is performed on the assembled columns + braces + gussets + anchor-bolts multi-body welded/bolted model with representative weld and bolt-preload stiffness; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Deliverables: STEP AP242, FEA per case, anchor-bolt tension table, modal, buckling eigenvalue, and mass report.
short_prompt:
Design the base frame of a Mobile Launch Tower for a small-launcher launch-site benchmark.

The tower supports a 50 t launch vehicle (fueled) at T-0 and must carry a 1 MN hold-down force per umbilical arm with four arms total (4 MN total hold-down). Tower overall height 30 m, base footprint 8 m x 8 m square. Base frame primary material: AS/NZS 3679.1 Grade 350 structural steel (Young's modulus 200 GPa, Poisson 0.30, yield 350 MPa, ultimate 450 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3) rolled sections. Four outer columns built from UC 310 x 283 kg/m W-sections; cross-bracing from CHS 200 x 8 mm tubes. Base anchor bolts: 12 x M48 Grade 10.9 into launch-site concrete mat.

Load cases per Eurocode EN 1990 + EN 1991-1-4 wind + ISO 19901: LC1 dead load 2450 kN vertical on base frame; LC2 pre-launch hold-down 4 MN upward + 2450 kN down = 1550 kN net upward; LC3 ignition transient 1 MN horizontal blast load for 500 ms; LC4 wind 50 m/s ULS 240 kN horizontal at mid-height; LC5 seismic 0.2 g horizontal; LC6 T-0 release 4 MN downward rebound on base for 100 ms.

prompt.title: Mobile launch tower base (multi-part welded/bolted steel assembly)
prompt.brief: Grade 350 steel base frame: 4 UC 310x283 columns + CHS 200x8 diagonal & horizontal bracing + 4-corner gusset plates + 4 umbilical-arm brackets + 12 M48 anchor bolts, 30 m tower for 50 t launcher with 4 MN hold-down.

parts.name: outer_column
parts.role: UC 310x283 kg/m W-section outer column at each of four tower corners

parts.name: diagonal_brace
parts.role: CHS 200 x 8 mm diagonal cross-brace tube between columns

parts.name: horizontal_brace
parts.role: CHS 200 x 8 mm horizontal base-level cross-brace between columns

parts.name: base_gusset_plate
parts.role: corner gusset plate welded between columns and braces at each of four base corners

parts.name: umbilical_arm_bracket
parts.role: tip attachment bracket at each of four umbilical-arm positions where 1 MN hold-down loads enter the tower

parts.name: anchor_bolt
parts.role: M48 Grade 10.9 anchor bolt tying the base frame to the launch-site concrete mat (12 bolts total)

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 8 m x 8 m base footprint x 30 m tower height
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X, Y ground plane, Z vertical; anchor bolts at base corners

boundary_conditions.interfaces: columns welded to gusset plates at base; diagonal and horizontal braces welded to columns; umbilical-arm brackets welded to column tops; anchor bolts preloaded through base gussets into concrete mat with representative preload stiffness
boundary_conditions.support: anchor-bolt pattern fixed to concrete mat in all LCs

prompt.objective: minimize base-frame mass under 50 t while passing yield, anchor-bolt, deflection, modal, and column-buckling gates

interfaces.load_path: horizontal base braces welded into columns
corner gusset plates welded between columns at base
diagonal braces welded into corner gusset plates
umbilical-arm brackets welded to column tops, 1 MN hold-down path into tower
anchor bolts preloaded through corner gusset plates into launch-site concrete mat

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Mobile launch tower bases are welded steel space-frames with four columns, diagonal and horizontal braces, corner gussets, umbilical-arm brackets, and anchor-bolt groups; hold-down, T-0 release, and anchor-bolt-tension gates explicitly cross weld and bolt interfaces that must be modelled as a multi-body frame.
notes.speculation_caveats: Brace count (8 diagonals + 4 horizontals) and gusset count are speculative; materials, loads, and thresholds inherit the original sample verbatim.
